note: For reference only. Does not replace clinical judgment, attending physician's opinion, or latest guidelines. Specific dosages must be individualized.

items.indications: Cardiac arrest, severe allergic reactions (anaphylactic shock), severe asthma exacerbation.
items.common_dose: Cardiac arrest: 1mg IV/IO, repeat every 3-5 minutes; Anaphylaxis: 0.3-0.5mg IM (anterolateral thigh).
items.route: IV, IO, IM, Endotracheal (ET, dose 2-2.5x higher)
items.warning: Concentration confusion is extremely dangerous! 1:1000 (1mg/ml) for IM anaphylaxis; 1:10000 (0.1mg/ml) for IV cardiac arrest.

items.indications: First-line vasoactive agent for various types of shock (especially septic and cardiogenic shock).
items.common_dose: Initial 0.05-0.1 mcg/kg/min, titrate to blood pressure, max typically not exceeding 1.0-2.0 mcg/kg/min.
items.warning: Extravasation causes severe tissue necrosis; avoid prolonged peripheral IV infusion. Phentolamine can be used for local infiltration if extravasation occurs.

items.indications: Bradycardia with hypotension, specific types of shock.
items.common_dose: 2-20 mcg/kg/min, titrate as needed. Low/med dose (2-10) emphasizes inotropic effect; high dose (>10) emphasizes vasopressor effect.
items.warning: Not recommended for routine septic shock (may increase arrhythmia risk); norepinephrine is preferred.

items.common_dose: Bradycardia: 1mg IV, repeat every 3-5 min, max total dose 3mg. Organophosphate poisoning requires individualized high-dose titration.
items.warning: Doses <0.5mg for bradycardia may cause paradoxical heart rate deceleration; use with caution in severe ischemia or MI.

items.indications: VF/pVT resuscitation, hemodynamically stable wide-QRS tachycardia.
items.common_dose: Cardiac arrest (post-defibrillation): 1st dose 300mg IV, 2nd dose 150mg. Stable arrhythmias: 150mg over 10 min, then 1mg/min for 6h, followed by 0.5mg/min.
items.warning: Rapid push easily causes severe hypotension and bradycardia. Must be diluted with 5% Dextrose (not normal saline).

items.indications: VF/pVT (as an alternative to amiodarone), premature ventricular contractions.
items.common_dose: Cardiac arrest: 1st dose 1-1.5mg/kg IV, repeat 0.5-0.75mg/kg in 5-10 min, max total dose 3mg/kg.
items.warning: Closely monitor for lidocaine toxicity (perioral numbness, seizures, altered mental status); reduce dose in hepatic impairment.

items.common_dose: Initial 5 mg/h, increase by 2.5 mg/h every 5-15 min, typically max 15 mg/h. Taper for maintenance after target is reached.
items.warning: Rapid onset; avoid excessively rapid blood pressure drops leading to cerebral, cardiac, or renal hypoperfusion.

items.indications: Reversal of opioid overdose and respiratory depression.
items.common_dose: Initial 0.4-2mg IV/IM/SC, repeat every 2-3 min. If no response after 10mg, consider other causes.
items.warning: In opioid-dependent patients, titrate carefully with small doses (start at 0.04-0.1mg) to avoid precipitating severe withdrawal symptoms.

items.indications: Hyperkalemia (with ECG changes), hypocalcemia, magnesium toxicity, calcium channel blocker overdose.
items.common_dose: 10% Calcium Gluconate 10-20ml IV, slow push (at least over 5-10 minutes). Can be repeated.
items.warning: Do not co-administer with sodium bicarbonate in the same line to prevent calcium carbonate precipitation. Do not push rapidly to avoid bradycardia or cardiac arrest.

items.indications: Severe metabolic acidosis, hyperkalemia, tricyclic antidepressant overdose.
items.common_dose: 1 mEq/kg IV (typically 50-100ml of 5% solution), adjust subsequently based on ABG.
items.warning: Ensure adequate ventilation to excrete generated CO2. Do not mix directly with calcium-containing fluids (e.g., Lactated Ringer's).
